segments.text: When Jesus realized that the Pharisees were aware He was gaining and baptizing more disciples than John
(although it was not Jesus who baptized, but His disciples),
He left Judea and returned to Galilee.
Now He had to pass through Samaria.
So He came to a town of Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of ground that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
Since Jacob’s well was there, Jesus, weary from His journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.
When a Samaritan woman came to draw water, Jesus said to her,
Give Me a drink.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (His disciples had gone into the town to buy food.)
You are a Jew,
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: said the woman.
How can You ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: (For Jews do not associate with Samaritans.)
Jesus answered,
If you knew the gift of God and who is asking you for a drink, you would have asked Him, and He would have given you living water.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Sir,
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: the woman replied,
You have nothing to draw with and the well is deep. Where then will You get this living water?
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Are You greater than our father Jacob, who gave us this well and drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock?
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Jesus said to her,
Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But whoever drinks the water I give him will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give him will become in him a fount of water springing up to eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The woman said to Him,
Sir, give me this water so that I will not get thirsty and have to keep coming here to draw water.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Jesus told her,
Go, call your husband and come back.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I have no husband,
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text:
the woman replied.
Jesus said to her,
You are correct to say that you have no husband.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: In fact, you have had five husbands, and the man you now have is not your husband. You have spoken truthfully.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Sir,
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: the woman said,
I see that You are a prophet.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say that the place where one must worship is in Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Believe Me, woman,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
a time is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we do know, for salvation is from the Jews.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But a time is coming and has now come when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and in truth, for the Father is seeking such as these to worship Him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: God is Spirit, and His worshipers must worship Him in spirit and in truth.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The woman said,
I know that Messiah
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: (called Christ)
is coming. When He comes, He will explain everything to us.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Jesus answered,
I who speak to you am He.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Just then His disciples returned and were surprised that He was speaking with a woman. But no one asked Him,
What do You want from her?
segments.speaker: no one

segments.text: or
Why are You talking with her?
segments.speaker: no one

segments.text: Then the woman left her water jar, went back into the town, and said to the people,
Come, see a man who told me everything I ever did. Could this be the Christ?
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: So they left the town and made their way toward Jesus.
Meanwhile the disciples urged Him,
Rabbi, eat something.
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: But He told them,
I have food to eat that you know nothing about.
segments.speaker: Meanwhile the disciples

segments.text: So the disciples asked one another,
Could someone have brought Him food?
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: Jesus explained,
My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me and to finish His work.
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: Do you not say, ‘There are still four months until the harvest’? I tell you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, for they are ripe for harvest.
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: Already the reaper draws his wages and gathers a crop for eternal life, so that the sower and the reaper may rejoice together.
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: For in this case the saying ‘One sows and another reaps’ is true.
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: I sent you to reap what you have not worked for; others have done the hard work, and now you have taken up their labor.
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: Many of the Samaritans from that town believed in Jesus because of the woman’s testimony,
He told me everything I ever did.
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: So when the Samaritans came to Him, they asked Him to stay with them, and He stayed two days.
And many more believed because of His message.
They said to the woman,
We now believe not only because of your words; we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this man truly is the Savior of the world.
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: After two days, Jesus left for Galilee.
Now He Himself had testified that a prophet has no honor in his own hometown.
Yet when He arrived, the Galileans welcomed Him. They had seen all the great things He had done in Jerusalem at the feast, for they had gone there as well.
So once again He came to Cana in Galilee, where He had turned the water into wine. And there was a royal official whose son lay sick at Capernaum.
When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and begged Him to come down and heal his son, who was about to die.
Jesus said to him,
Unless you people see signs and wonders, you will never believe.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Sir,
segments.speaker: Nobleman

segments.text: the official said,
come down before my child dies.
segments.speaker: The official

segments.text: Go,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: said Jesus.
Your son will live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The man took Jesus at His word and departed.
And while he was still on the way, his servants met him with the news that his boy was alive.
So he inquired as to the hour when his son had recovered, and they told him,
The fever left him yesterday at the seventh hour.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the father realized that this was the very hour in which Jesus had told him,
Your son will live.
segments.speaker: the Father

segments.text: And he and all his household believed.
This was now the second sign that Jesus performed after coming from Judea into Galilee.
